cards.back: A global state is the current state of a distributed system, where all nodes in the system have the same view of the state.
A global state is a snapshot of the entire system, where all nodes agree on the current state of the system.
There are two types of global states: 1) Atomicity and 2) Consistency in distributed systems.
A simple example of global state is a bank account balance. All nodes in the system should agree on the current balance.
Global state is crucial in distributed systems as it ensures consistency and accuracy of data across all nodes in the system.
